description_short: Charlotte is the largest city in North Carolina and faces tornadoes, severe thunderstorms, and occasional winter ice storms across the Piedmont.
description_long: Located in the Carolina Piedmont near the South Carolina border, Charlotte experiences severe thunderstorms and tornadoes during the spring and fall, with a secondary winter threat from ice storms and cold snaps. Heavy rain from tropical systems can bring inland flooding. Mecklenburg County is served by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg (office code GSP) within forecast zone NCZ071 (Mecklenburg).
